    55	var _ = Describe("RouteAgent syncing", func() {
    56	        t := newTestDriver()
    57	
    58	        It("should create a RouteAgent resource", func() {
    59	                t.awaitRouteAgent(nil)
    60	        })
    61	
    62	        When("a remote Endpoint is created/updated/deleted", func() {
    63	                It("should add/update/delete its RemoteEndpoint information in the RouteAgent resource", func() {
    64	                        endpoint := t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
    65	
    66	                        t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(func(ep *submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, g Gomega) {
    67	                                g.Expect(ep.Spec).To(Equal(endpoint.Spec))
    68	                        })
    69	
    70	                        By("Updating remote endpoint")
    71	
    72	                        endpoint.Spec.Hostname = "newHostName"
    73	                        t.UpdateEndpoint(endpoint)
    74	
    75	                        t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(func(ep *submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, g Gomega) {
    76	                                g.Expect(ep.Spec.Hostname).To(Equal(endpoint.Spec.Hostname))
    77	                                g.Expect(ep.Spec).To(Equal(endpoint.Spec))
    78	                        })
    79	
    80	                        By("Deleting remote endpoint")
    81	
    82	                        t.DeleteEndpoint(endpoint.Name)
    83	
    84	                        t.awaitRouteAgent(func(ra *submarinerv1.RouteAgent, g Gomega) {
    85	                                g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints).To(BeEmpty())
    86	                        })
    87	                })
    88	        })
    89	
    90	        When("a stale remote Endpoint is deleted", func() {
    91	                It("should remove its RemoteEndpoint information in the RouteAgent resource", func() {
    92	                        endpoint1 := t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
    93	
    94	                        t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(nil)
    95	
    96	                        By("Creating new remote endpoint")
    97	
    98	                        endpoint2 := t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP2)
    99	                        endpoint2.Spec.CableName = "new-cable"
   100	                        endpoint2.Name = "new-endpoint"
   101	                        endpoint2.CreationTimestamp = metav1.Time{Time: metav1.Now().Add(time.Second)}
   102	                        t.CreateEndpoint(endpoint2)
   103	
   104	                        t.awaitRouteAgent(func(ra *submarinerv1.RouteAgent, g Gomega) {
   105	                                g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints).To(HaveLen(2))
   106	                        })
   107	
   108	                        By("Deleting stale remote endpoint")
   109	
   110	                        t.DeleteEndpoint(endpoint1.Name)
   111	
   112	                        t.awaitRouteAgent(func(ra *submarinerv1.RouteAgent, g Gomega) {
   113	                                g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints).To(HaveLen(1))
   114	                                g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints[0].Spec.GetHealthCheckIP(k8snet.IPv4)).To(Equal(healthCheckIP2))
   115	                        })
   116	                })
   117	        })
   118	
   119	        When("the RouteAgent resource exists on startup without the OwnerReference", func() {
   120	                BeforeEach(func() {
   121	                        _, err := t.routeAgents.Create(context.TODO(), &submarinerv1.RouteAgent{
   122	                                ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
   123	                                        Name:      localNodeName,
   124	                                        Namespace: namespace,
   125	                                },
   126	                        }, metav1.CreateOptions{})
   127	                        Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())
   128	                })
   129	
   130	                It("should add the OwnerReference", func() {
   131	                        t.awaitRouteAgent(nil)
   132	                })
   133	        })
   134	})
   135	
   136	var _ = Describe("RemoteEndpoint latency info", func() {
   137	        t := newTestDriver()
   138	
   139	        When("a remote Endpoint is created", func() {
   140	                It("should start a pinger and correctly update the RemoteEndpoint Status and LatencyInfo", func() {
   141	                        t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   142	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStart()
   143	
   144	                        latencyInfo := t.newLatencyInfo(k8snet.IPv4)
   145	                        t.setLatencyInfo(healthCheckIP1, latencyInfo)
   146	
   147	                        t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(func(ep *submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, g Gomega) {
   148	                                g.Expect(ep.Status).To(Equal(submarinerv1.Connected))
   149	                                g.Expect(ep.LatencyRTT).To(Equal(latencyInfo.Spec))
   150	                        })
   151	                })
   152	
   153	                Context("with no HealthCheckIP", func() {
   154	                        It("should not start a pinger and should set the RemoteEndpoint Status to None", func() {
   155	                                endpoint := t.newSubmEndpoint()
   156	                                endpoint.Spec.Subnets = []string{"2.2.2.2/24"}
   157	                                t.CreateEndpoint(endpoint)
   158	                                t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitNoStart()
   159	
   160	                                t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(func(ep *submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, g Gomega) {
   161	                                        g.Expect(ep.Status).To(Equal(submarinerv1.ConnectionNone))
   162	                                        g.Expect(ep.Spec).To(Equal(endpoint.Spec))
   163	                                })
   164	                        })
   165	                })
   166	
   167	                Context("on the gateway", func() {
   168	                        It("should not start a pinger and should set the RemoteEndpoint Status to None", func() {
   169	                                _ = t.CreateLocalHostEndpoint()
   170	                                endpoint1 := t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   171	                                t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitNoStart()
   172	
   173	                                t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(func(ep *submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, g Gomega) {
   174	                                        g.Expect(ep.Status).To(Equal(submarinerv1.ConnectionNone))
   175	                                        g.Expect(ep.Spec).To(Equal(endpoint1.Spec))
   176	                                })
   177	                        })
   178	                })
   179	
   180	                Context("with health check not enabled", func() {
   181	                        BeforeEach(func() {
   182	                                t.healthcheckerEnabled = false
   183	                        })
   184	
   185	                        It("should not start a pinger and should set the RemoteEndpoint Status to None", func() {
   186	                                endpoint1 := t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   187	                                t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitNoStart()
   188	
   189	                                t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(func(ep *submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, g Gomega) {
   190	                                        g.Expect(ep.Status).To(Equal(submarinerv1.ConnectionNone))
   191	                                        g.Expect(ep.Spec).To(Equal(endpoint1.Spec))
   192	                                })
   193	                        })
   194	                })
   195	        })
   196	
   197	        When("a remote Endpoint is updated and the HealthCheckIP was changed", func() {
   198	                It("should stop the pinger and start a new one", func() {
   199	                        endpoint1 := t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   200	
   201	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStart()
   202	
   203	                        endpoint1.Spec.HealthCheckIPs = []string{healthCheckIP2}
   204	
   205	                        t.UpdateEndpoint(endpoint1)
   206	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStop()
   207	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP2].AwaitStart()
   208	                })
   209	        })
   210	
   211	        When("a remote Endpoint is deleted", func() {
   212	                It("should stop the pinger", func() {
   213	                        endpoint1 := t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   214	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStart()
   215	
   216	                        t.DeleteEndpoint(endpoint1.Name)
   217	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStop()
   218	                })
   219	        })
   220	
   221	        When("a remote Endpoint with dual-stack health check IPs is created/deleted", func() {
   222	                const healthCheckIPv6 = "2001:db8:3333:4444:5555:6666:7777:8888"
   223	
   224	                BeforeEach(func() {
   225	                        t.supportedIPFamilies = []k8snet.IPFamily{k8snet.IPv4, k8snet.IPv6}
   226	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIPv6] = fake.NewPinger(healthCheckIPv6)
   227	                })
   228	
   229	                It("should start/stop Pingers and return the correct LatencyInfo for both", func() {
   230	                        endpoint := t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1, healthCheckIPv6)
   231	                        endpoint.Spec.PublicIPs = []string{"2002:0:0:1234::", "2.2.2.2"}
   232	                        endpoint.Spec.PrivateIPs = []string{"2003:0:0:1234::", "3.3.3.3"}
   233	
   234	                        t.CreateEndpoint(endpoint)
   235	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStart()
   236	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIPv6].AwaitStart()
   237	
   238	                        ipv4LatencyInfo := t.newLatencyInfo(k8snet.IPv4)
   239	                        t.setLatencyInfo(healthCheckIP1, ipv4LatencyInfo)
   240	
   241	                        ipv6LatencyInfo := t.newLatencyInfo(k8snet.IPv6)
   242	                        t.setLatencyInfo(healthCheckIPv6, ipv6LatencyInfo)
   243	
   244	                        t.awaitRouteAgent(func(ra *submarinerv1.RouteAgent, g Gomega) {
   245	                                epMap := map[string]*submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint{}
   246	
   247	                                for i := range ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints {
   248	                                        g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints[i].Spec.HealthCheckIPs).To(HaveLen(1))
   249	                                        epMap[ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints[i].Spec.HealthCheckIPs[0]] = &ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints[i]
   250	                                }
   251	
   252	                                ipv4Endpoint := epMap[healthCheckIP1]
   253	                                g.Expect(ipv4Endpoint).ToNot(BeNil(), "RemoteEndpoint not found for IPv4 health check IP %q", healthCheckIP1)
   254	                                g.Expect(ipv4Endpoint.Status).To(Equal(submarinerv1.Connected))
   255	                                g.Expect(ipv4Endpoint.LatencyRTT).To(Equal(ipv4LatencyInfo.Spec))
   256	
   257	                                spec := endpoint.Spec
   258	                                spec.HealthCheckIPs = []string{healthCheckIP1}
   259	                                spec.PublicIPs = []string{endpoint.Spec.GetPublicIP(k8snet.IPv4)}
   260	                                spec.PrivateIPs = []string{endpoint.Spec.GetPrivateIP(k8snet.IPv4)}
   261	                                g.Expect(ipv4Endpoint.Spec).To(Equal(spec))
   262	
   263	                                ipv6Endpoint := epMap[healthCheckIPv6]
   264	                                g.Expect(ipv6Endpoint).ToNot(BeNil(), "RemoteEndpoint not found for IPv6 health check IP %q", healthCheckIP1)
   265	                                g.Expect(ipv6Endpoint.Status).To(Equal(submarinerv1.Connected))
   266	                                g.Expect(ipv6Endpoint.LatencyRTT).To(Equal(ipv6LatencyInfo.Spec))
   267	
   268	                                spec = endpoint.Spec
   269	                                spec.HealthCheckIPs = []string{healthCheckIPv6}
   270	                                spec.PublicIPs = []string{endpoint.Spec.GetPublicIP(k8snet.IPv6)}
   271	                                spec.PrivateIPs = []string{endpoint.Spec.GetPrivateIP(k8snet.IPv6)}
   272	                                g.Expect(ipv6Endpoint.Spec).To(Equal(spec))
   273	
   274	                                g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints).To(HaveLen(2))
   275	                        })
   276	
   277	                        By("Deleting Endpoint")
   278	
   279	                        t.DeleteEndpoint(endpoint.Name)
   280	
   281	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStop()
   282	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIPv6].AwaitStop()
   283	
   284	                        t.awaitRouteAgent(func(ra *submarinerv1.RouteAgent, g Gomega) {
   285	                                g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints).To(BeEmpty())
   286	                        })
   287	                })
   288	        })
   289	
   290	        When("a pinger reports a connection error", func() {
   291	                It(" should set the RemoteEndpoint Status to Error", func() {
   292	                        t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   293	
   294	                        latencyInfo := &pinger.LatencyInfo{
   295	                                ConnectionStatus: pinger.ConnectionError,
   296	                                ConnectionError:  "pinger failed",
   297	                        }
   298	
   299	                        t.setLatencyInfo(healthCheckIP1, latencyInfo)
   300	
   301	                        t.awaitRemoteEndpoint(func(ep *submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, g Gomega) {
   302	                                g.Expect(ep.Status).To(Equal(submarinerv1.ConnectionError))
   303	                                g.Expect(ep.StatusMessage).To(Equal(latencyInfo.ConnectionError))
   304	                        })
   305	                })
   306	        })
   307	})
   308	
   309	var _ = Describe("Gateway transition", func() {
   310	        t := newTestDriver()
   311	
   312	        Context("to gateway node", func() {
   313	                It("should stop the pinger", func() {
   314	                        _ = t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   315	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStart()
   316	
   317	                        _ = t.CreateLocalHostEndpoint()
   318	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStop()
   319	                })
   320	        })
   321	
   322	        Context("to non-gateway node", func() {
   323	                It("should start the pinger", func() {
   324	                        endpoint := t.CreateLocalHostEndpoint()
   325	                        _ = t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   326	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitNoStart()
   327	
   328	                        t.DeleteEndpoint(endpoint.Name)
   329	                        t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStart()
   330	                })
   331	        })
   332	})
   333	
   334	var _ = Describe("Stop", func() {
   335	        t := newTestDriver()
   336	
   337	        It("should stop the Pingers and delete the RouteAgent resource", func() {
   338	                t.CreateEndpoint(t.newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIP1))
   339	                t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStart()
   340	
   341	                t.awaitRouteAgent(nil)
   342	
   343	                Expect(t.handler.Stop()).To(Succeed())
   344	
   345	                t.pingerMap[healthCheckIP1].AwaitStop()
   346	
   347	                Eventually(func(g Gomega) {
   348	                        _, err := t.routeAgents.Get(context.TODO(), localNodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   349	                        g.Expect(apierrors.IsNotFound(err)).To(BeTrue())
   350	                }).Within(5 * time.Second).Should(Succeed())
   351	
   352	                Expect(t.handler.Stop()).To(Succeed())
   353	        })
   354	})
   355	
   356	type testDriver struct {
   357	        *eventtesting.ControllerSupport
   358	        supportedIPFamilies  []k8snet.IPFamily
   359	        pingerMap            map[string]*fake.Pinger
   360	        handler              event.Handler
   361	        endpoints            dynamic.ResourceInterface
   362	        routeAgents          submarinerv1client.RouteAgentInterface
   363	        submClient           *fakeClient.Clientset
   364	        healthcheckerEnabled bool
   365	}
   366	
   367	func newTestDriver() *testDriver {
   368	        t := &testDriver{
   369	                ControllerSupport: eventtesting.NewControllerSupport(),
   370	        }
   371	
   372	        BeforeEach(func() {
   373	                t.supportedIPFamilies = []k8snet.IPFamily{k8snet.IPv4}
   374	                t.healthcheckerEnabled = true
   375	
   376	                t.submClient = fakeClient.NewSimpleClientset()
   377	
   378	                dynamicClient := dynamicfake.NewSimpleDynamicClient(kubeScheme.Scheme)
   379	
   380	                t.endpoints = dynamicClient.Resource(submarinerv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithResource("endpoints")).Namespace(namespace)
   381	                t.routeAgents = t.submClient.SubmarinerV1().RouteAgents(namespace)
   382	                t.pingerMap = map[string]*fake.Pinger{
   383	                        healthCheckIP1: fake.NewPinger(healthCheckIP1),
   384	                        healthCheckIP2: fake.NewPinger(healthCheckIP2),
   385	                }
   386	        })
   387	
   388	        JustBeforeEach(func() {
   389	                config := &healthchecker.Config{
   390	                        ControllerConfig: pinger.ControllerConfig{
   391	                                SupportedIPFamilies: t.supportedIPFamilies,
   392	                                PingInterval:        1, // Set interval to 1 second for faster testing
   393	                                MaxPacketLossCount:  1,
   394	                                NewPinger: func(pingerCfg pinger.Config) pinger.Interface {
   395	                                        defer GinkgoRecover()
   396	
   397	                                        p, ok := t.pingerMap[pingerCfg.IP]
   398	                                        Expect(ok).To(BeTrue())
   399	
   400	                                        return p
   401	                                },
   402	                        },
   403	                        HealthCheckerEnabled:     t.healthcheckerEnabled,
   404	                        RouteAgentUpdateInterval: 100 * time.Millisecond,
   405	                        LocalNodeName:            localNodeName,
   406	                        Version:                  "v1",
   407	                        Namespace:                namespace,
   408	                        RouteAgentOwner: &corev1.Pod{
   409	                                ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
   410	                                        Name:      ownerName,
   411	                                        Namespace: namespace,
   412	                                        UID:       uuid.NewUUID(),
   413	                                },
   414	                        },
   415	                }
   416	
   417	                t.handler = healthchecker.New(config, t.submClient.SubmarinerV1())
   418	
   419	                t.Start(t.handler)
   420	        })
   421	
   422	        return t
   423	}
   424	
   425	func (t *testDriver) newSubmEndpoint(healthCheckIPs ...string) *submarinerv1.Endpoint {
   426	        endpointSpec := &submarinerv1.EndpointSpec{
   427	                ClusterID:      remoteClusterID,
   428	                CableName:      fmt.Sprintf("submariner-cable-%s-192-68-1-20", remoteClusterID),
   429	                HealthCheckIPs: healthCheckIPs,
   430	        }
   431	
   432	        for _, ip := range healthCheckIPs {
   433	                endpointSpec.Subnets = append(endpointSpec.Subnets, ip+"/24")
   434	        }
   435	
   436	        endpointName, err := endpointSpec.GenerateName()
   437	        Expect(err).To(Succeed())
   438	
   439	        endpoint := &submarinerv1.Endpoint{
   440	                ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
   441	                        Name:              endpointName,
   442	                        CreationTimestamp: metav1.Now(),
   443	                },
   444	                Spec: *endpointSpec,
   445	        }
   446	
   447	        return endpoint
   448	}
   449	
   450	func (t *testDriver) newLatencyInfo(family k8snet.IPFamily) *pinger.LatencyInfo {
   451	        return &pinger.LatencyInfo{
   452	                ConnectionStatus: pinger.Connected,
   453	                Spec: &submarinerv1.LatencyRTTSpec{
   454	                        Last:    string(family) + "82ms",
   455	                        Min:     string(family) + "80ms",
   456	                        Average: string(family) + "85ms",
   457	                        Max:     string(family) + "89ms",
   458	                        StdDev:  string(family) + "5ms",
   459	                },
   460	        }
   461	}
   462	
   463	func (t *testDriver) setLatencyInfo(ip string, latencyInfo *pinger.LatencyInfo) {
   464	        pingerObject := t.pingerMap[ip]
   465	        pingerObject.SetLatencyInfo(latencyInfo)
   466	}
   467	
   468	func (t *testDriver) Start(handler event.Handler) {
   469	        t.ControllerSupport.Start(handler)
   470	}
   471	
   472	func (t *testDriver) awaitRouteAgent(verify func(*submarinerv1.RouteAgent, Gomega)) {
   473	        Eventually(func(g Gomega) {
   474	                ra, err := t.routeAgents.Get(context.TODO(), localNodeName, metav1.GetOptions{})
   475	                g.Expect(err).ToNot(HaveOccurred(), "Error retrieving RouteAgent")
   476	
   477	                g.Expect(ra.OwnerReferences).To(HaveLen(1))
   478	                g.Expect(ra.OwnerReferences[0].Name).To(Equal(ownerName))
   479	                g.Expect(ra.OwnerReferences[0].Kind).To(Equal("Pod"))
   480	
   481	                if verify != nil {
   482	                        verify(ra, g)
   483	                }
   484	        }).Within(5 * time.Second).Should(Succeed())
   485	}
   486	
   487	func (t *testDriver) awaitRemoteEndpoint(verify func(*submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint, Gomega)) {
   488	        t.awaitRouteAgent(func(ra *submarinerv1.RouteAgent, g Gomega) {
   489	                g.Expect(ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints).ToNot(BeEmpty())
   490	
   491	                if verify != nil {
   492	                        verify(&ra.Status.RemoteEndpoints[0], g)
   493	                }
   494	        })
   495	}
   496

    45	
    46	type Config struct {
    47	        pinger.ControllerConfig
    48	        HealthCheckerEnabled     bool
    49	        RouteAgentUpdateInterval time.Duration
    50	        RouteAgentOwner          metav1.Object
    51	        Namespace                string
    52	        LocalNodeName            string
    53	        Version                  string
    54	}
    55	
    56	type controller struct {
    57	        event.HandlerBase
    58	        pingerController pinger.Controller
    59	        config           Config
    60	        stopCh           chan struct{}
    61	        stopOnce         sync.Once
    62	        client           v1typed.RouteAgentInterface
    63	}
    64	
    65	var logger = log.Logger{Logger: logf.Log.WithName("HealthChecker")}
    66	
    67	func New(config *Config, client v1typed.SubmarinerV1Interface) event.Handler {
    68	        return &controller{
    69	                pingerController: pinger.NewController(config.ControllerConfig),
    70	                config:           *config,
    71	                client:           client.RouteAgents(config.Namespace),
    72	                stopCh:           make(chan struct{}),
    73	        }
    74	}
    75	
    76	func (h *controller) Stop() error {
    77	        h.pingerController.Stop()
    78	
    79	        h.stopOnce.Do(func() {
    80	                close(h.stopCh)
    81	        })
    82	
    83	        err := h.client.Delete(context.TODO(),
    84	                h.config.LocalNodeName, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
    85	        if err != nil && !apiError.IsNotFound(err) {
    86	                return errors.Wrapf(err, "Error deleting RouteAgent: %s", h.config.LocalNodeName)
    87	        }
    88	
    89	        return nil
    90	}
    91	
    92	func (h *controller) RemoteEndpointCreated(endpoint *submarinerv1.Endpoint) error {
    93	        return h.onRemoteEndpint(endpoint, "created")
    94	}
    95	
    96	func (h *controller) RemoteEndpointUpdated(endpoint *submarinerv1.Endpoint) error {
    97	        return h.onRemoteEndpint(endpoint, "updated")
    98	}
    99	
   100	func (h *controller) onRemoteEndpint(endpoint *submarinerv1.Endpoint, op string) error {
   101	        if !h.config.HealthCheckerEnabled || h.State().IsOnGateway() {
   102	                return nil
   103	        }
   104	
   105	        h.processEndpointCreatedOrUpdated(endpoint, op)
   106	
   107	        return nil
   108	}
   109	
   110	func (h *controller) processEndpointCreatedOrUpdated(endpoint *submarinerv1.Endpoint, op string) {
   111	        logger.Infof("Processing %s Endpoint: %s", op, resource.ToJSON(endpoint))
   112	        h.pingerController.EndpointCreatedOrUpdated(&endpoint.Spec)
   113	}
   114	
   115	func (h *controller) RemoteEndpointRemoved(endpoint *submarinerv1.Endpoint) error {
   116	        logger.Infof("Processing removed Endpoint %q", endpoint.Spec.CableName)
   117	        h.pingerController.EndpointRemoved(&endpoint.Spec)
   118	
   119	        return nil
   120	}
   121	
   122	func (h *controller) StaleRemoteEndpointRemoved(endpoint *submarinerv1.Endpoint) error {
   123	        logger.Infof("Processing stale removed Endpoint: %s", resource.ToJSON(endpoint))
   124	        h.pingerController.EndpointRemoved(&endpoint.Spec)
   125	
   126	        return nil
   127	}
   128	
   129	func (h *controller) Init(_ context.Context) error {
   130	        //nolint:contextcheck // Ignore "should pass the context parameter"
   131	        go func() {
   132	                wait.Until(func() {
   133	                        h.syncRouteAgentStatus()
   134	                }, h.config.RouteAgentUpdateInterval, h.stopCh)
   135	        }()
   136	
   137	        return nil
   138	}
   139	
   140	// TransitionToNonGateway is called once for each transition of the local node from Gateway to a non-Gateway.
   141	func (h *controller) TransitionToNonGateway() error {
   142	        if h.config.HealthCheckerEnabled {
   143	                remoteEndpoints := h.State().GetRemoteEndpoints()
   144	
   145	                for i := range remoteEndpoints {
   146	                        h.processEndpointCreatedOrUpdated(&remoteEndpoints[i], "existing")
   147	                }
   148	        }
   149	
   150	        return nil
   151	}
   152	
   153	// TransitionToGateway is called once for each transition of the local node from non-Gateway to a Gateway.
   154	func (h *controller) TransitionToGateway() error {
   155	        if h.config.HealthCheckerEnabled {
   156	                h.pingerController.Stop()
   157	        }
   158	
   159	        h.syncRouteAgentStatus()
   160	
   161	        return nil
   162	}
   163	
   164	func (h *controller) GetNetworkPlugins() []string {
   165	        return []string{event.AnyNetworkPlugin}
   166	}
   167	
   168	func (h *controller) GetName() string {
   169	        return "routeAgent-health-checker"
   170	}
   171	
   172	func (h *controller) createRemoteEndpoint(endpointSpec *submarinerv1.EndpointSpec, family k8snet.IPFamily) submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint {
   173	        var (
   174	                connectionStatus submarinerv1.ConnectionStatus
   175	                statusMessage    string
   176	                latencyRTT       *submarinerv1.LatencyRTTSpec
   177	        )
   178	
   179	        if !h.config.HealthCheckerEnabled {
   180	                connectionStatus = submarinerv1.ConnectionNone
   181	                statusMessage = "Health check is not enabled"
   182	        } else if h.State().IsOnGateway() {
   183	                connectionStatus = submarinerv1.ConnectionNone
   184	                statusMessage = "Health check is not performed on gateway nodes"
   185	        } else if pingerObject := h.pingerController.Get(endpointSpec, family); pingerObject != nil {
   186	                latencyInfo := pingerObject.GetLatencyInfo()
   187	                if latencyInfo != nil {
   188	                        switch latencyInfo.ConnectionStatus {
   189	                        case pinger.Connected:
   190	                                connectionStatus = submarinerv1.Connected
   191	                                statusMessage = ""
   192	                                latencyRTT = &submarinerv1.LatencyRTTSpec{
   193	                                        Last:    latencyInfo.Spec.Last,
   194	                                        Min:     latencyInfo.Spec.Min,
   195	                                        Average: latencyInfo.Spec.Average,
   196	                                        Max:     latencyInfo.Spec.Max,
   197	                                        StdDev:  latencyInfo.Spec.StdDev,
   198	                                }
   199	                        case pinger.ConnectionError, pinger.ConnectionUnknown:
   200	                                connectionStatus = submarinerv1.ConnectionError
   201	                                statusMessage = latencyInfo.ConnectionError
   202	                        }
   203	                } else {
   204	                        connectionStatus = submarinerv1.Connecting
   205	                        statusMessage = ""
   206	                }
   207	        } else {
   208	                connectionStatus = submarinerv1.ConnectionNone
   209	                statusMessage = fmt.Sprintf("IPv%s health check IP is not configured", family)
   210	        }
   211	
   212	        remoteEndpoint := submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint{
   213	                Status:        connectionStatus,
   214	                StatusMessage: statusMessage,
   215	                Spec:          *endpointSpec,
   216	                LatencyRTT:    latencyRTT,
   217	        }
   218	
   219	        toPluralIPs := func(get func(family k8snet.IPFamily) string) []string {
   220	                ip := get(family)
   221	                if ip != "" {
   222	                        return []string{ip}
   223	                }
   224	
   225	                return nil
   226	        }
   227	
   228	        remoteEndpoint.Spec.HealthCheckIPs = toPluralIPs(endpointSpec.GetHealthCheckIP)
   229	        remoteEndpoint.Spec.PublicIPs = toPluralIPs(endpointSpec.GetPublicIP)
   230	        remoteEndpoint.Spec.PrivateIPs = toPluralIPs(endpointSpec.GetPrivateIP)
   231	
   232	        return remoteEndpoint
   233	}
   234	
   235	func (h *controller) syncRouteAgentStatus() {
   236	        routeAgent := h.generateRouteAgentObject()
   237	        remoteEndpoints := h.State().GetRemoteEndpoints()
   238	
   239	        for i := range remoteEndpoints {
   240	                for _, family := range h.config.SupportedIPFamilies {
   241	                        if slices.Contains(remoteEndpoints[i].Spec.GetIPFamilies(), family) {
   242	                                routeAgent.Status.RemoteEndpoints = append(routeAgent.Status.RemoteEndpoints,
   243	                                        h.createRemoteEndpoint(&remoteEndpoints[i].Spec, family))
   244	                        }
   245	                }
   246	        }
   247	
   248	        // Use CreateOrUpdate to handle the RouteAgent resource
   249	        _, err := util.CreateOrUpdate(context.TODO(), h.routeAgentResourceInterface(), routeAgent,
   250	                func(existing *submarinerv1.RouteAgent) (*submarinerv1.RouteAgent, error) {
   251	                        existing.OwnerReferences = routeAgent.OwnerReferences
   252	                        existing.Status = routeAgent.Status
   253	
   254	                        return existing, nil
   255	                })
   256	        if err != nil {
   257	                utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("error creating/updating RouteAgent: %w", err))
   258	                return
   259	        }
   260	}
   261	
   262	func (h *controller) generateRouteAgentObject() *submarinerv1.RouteAgent {
   263	        ra := &submarinerv1.RouteAgent{
   264	                ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
   265	                        Name:      h.config.LocalNodeName,
   266	                        Namespace: h.config.Namespace,
   267	                },
   268	                Status: submarinerv1.RouteAgentStatus{
   269	                        Version:         h.config.Version,
   270	                        StatusFailure:   "",
   271	                        RemoteEndpoints: []submarinerv1.RemoteEndpoint{},
   272	                },
   273	        }
   274	
   275	        err := controllerutil.SetOwnerReference(h.config.RouteAgentOwner, ra, scheme.Scheme)
   276	        if err != nil {
   277	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error setting owner reference")
   278	        }
   279	
   280	        return ra
   281	}
   282	
   283	func (h *controller) routeAgentResourceInterface() resource.Interface[*submarinerv1.RouteAgent] {
   284	        return &resource.InterfaceFuncs[*submarinerv1.RouteAgent]{
   285	                GetFunc:    h.client.Get,
   286	                CreateFunc: h.client.Create,
   287	                UpdateFunc: h.client.Update,
   288	                DeleteFunc: h.client.Delete,
   289	        }
   290	}

    83	        err := h.client.Delete(ctx,
    84	                h.config.LocalNodeName, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
    85	        if err != nil && !apiError.IsNotFound(err) {
    86	                return errors.Wrapf(err, "Error deleting RouteAgent: %s", h.config.LocalNodeName)
    87	        }
    88	
    89	        return nil
    90	}

    36	func (ovn *Handler) Stop() error {
    37	        ovn.gatewayRouteController.stop()
    38	
    39	        if ovn.nonGatewayRouteController != nil {
    40	                ovn.nonGatewayRouteController.stop()
    41	        }
    42	
    43	        close(ovn.stopCh)
    44	
    45	        return nil
    46	}
    47	
    48	func (ovn *Handler) Uninstall() error {
    49	        logger.Infof("Uninstalling OVN components from the node")
    50	
    51	        err := ovn.cleanupRoutes()
    52	        if err != nil {
    53	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error cleaning the routes")
    54	        }
    55	
    56	        err = ovn.netLink.FlushRouteTable(constants.RouteAgentInterClusterNetworkTableID)
    57	        if err != nil {
    58	                logger.Errorf(err, "Flushing routing table %d returned error",
    59	                        constants.RouteAgentInterClusterNetworkTableID)
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        err = ovn.netLink.FlushRouteTable(constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID)
    63	        if err != nil {
    64	                logger.Errorf(err, "Flushing routing table %d returned error",
    65	                        constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID)
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        ovn.deleteIPHookChain(packetfilter.TableTypeFilter, chains.NewForwarding())
    69	        ovn.deleteIPHookChain(packetfilter.TableTypeFilter, chains.NewForwardingMSSClamp())
    70	        ovn.deleteIPHookChain(packetfilter.TableTypeNAT, chains.NewPostRouting())
    71	
    72	        err = util.Update[*corev1.Node](context.TODO(), ovn.nodeResourceInterface(), &corev1.Node{
    73	                ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: nodeutil.GetLocalNodeName()},
    74	        }, func(existing *corev1.Node) (*corev1.Node, error) {
    75	                delete(existing.Annotations, OVNKSNATExcludeSubnetsAnnotation)
    76	                return existing, nil
    77	        })
    78	        if err != nil {
    79	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error removing node annotation %q", OVNKSNATExcludeSubnetsAnnotation)
    80	        }
    81	
    82	        return nil
    83	}
    84	
    85	func (ovn *Handler) deleteIPHookChain(table packetfilter.TableType, chain *packetfilter.ChainIPHook) {
    86	        if err := ovn.pFilter.ClearChain(table, chain.Name); err != nil {
    87	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error clearing IP hook chain %q from %q table", chain.Name, table)
    88	        }
    89	
    90	        if err := ovn.pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(chain); err != nil {
    91	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IP hook chain %q from %q table", chain.Name, table)
    92	        }
    93	}
    94	
    95	func (ovn *Handler) cleanupRoutes() error {
    96	        rules, err := ovn.netLink.RuleList(k8snet.IPv4)
    97	        if err != nil {
    98	                return errors.Wrapf(err, "error listing rules")
    99	        }
   100	
   101	        for i := range rules {
   102	                if rules[i].Table == constants.RouteAgentInterClusterNetworkTableID || rules[i].Table == constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID {
   103	                        err = ovn.netLink.RuleDel(&rules[i])
   104	                        if err != nil {
   105	                                return errors.Wrapf(err, "error deleting the rule %v", rules[i])
   106	                        }
   107	                }
   108	        }
   109	
   110	        return nil
   111	}
   112	
   113	func (ovn *Handler) LegacyCleanup() {
   114	        err := vsctl.DelInternalPort(ovnK8sSubmarinerBridge, ovnK8sSubmarinerInterface)
   115	        if err != nil {
   116	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting Submariner port %q", ovnK8sSubmarinerInterface)
   117	        }

   121	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting Submariner bridge %q", ovnK8sSubmarinerBridge)

   266	func (h *mtuHandler) forceMssClamping(endpoint *submV1.Endpoint) error {
   267	        tcpMssSrc := "user"
   268	        tcpMssValue := h.tcpMssValue
   269	
   270	        if tcpMssValue == 0 {
   271	                defaultHostIface, err := netlinkAPI.New().GetDefaultGatewayInterface(h.ipFamily)
   272	                if err != nil {
   273	                        return errors.Wrapf(err, "Unable to find the default interface on host")
   274	                }

   276	                overHeadSize := MaxIPSecOverhead
   277	                if endpoint.Spec.Backend == vxlandriver.CableDriverName {
   278	                        overHeadSize = vxlan.MTUOverhead
   279	                }

    30	        "github.com/vishvananda/netlink"

    32	
    33	func (kp *SyncHandler) Uninstall() error {
    34	        logger.Infof("Uninstalling Submariner changes from the node %q", kp.hostname)
    35	        logger.Infof("Flushing route table %d entries", constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID)
    36	
    37	        err := kp.netLink.FlushRouteTable(constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID)
    38	        if err != nil {
    39	                // We can safely ignore this error, as this table will exist only on GW nodes
    40	                logger.V(log.TRACE).Infof("Flushing routing table %d returned error. Can be ignored on non-Gw node: %v",
    41	                        constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID, err)
    42	        }
    43	
    44	        err = kp.netLink.RuleDelIfPresent(netlinkAPI.NewTableRule(constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID, kp.ipFamily))
    45	        if err != nil {
    46	                logger.V(log.TRACE).Infof("Deleting IP Rule pointing to %d table returned error: %v",
    47	                        constants.RouteAgentHostNetworkTableID, err)
    48	        }
    49	
    50	        kp.deleteVxLANInterface()
    51	        kp.deleteIPTableChains()
    52	
    53	        return nil
    54	}
    55	
    56	func (kp *SyncHandler) deleteVxLANInterface() {
    57	        iface := &netlink.Vxlan{
    58	                LinkAttrs: netlink.LinkAttrs{
    59	                        Name:  kp.vxlanIface,
    60	                        Flags: net.FlagUp,
    61	                },
    62	                VxlanId: 100,
    63	                SrcAddr: nil,
    64	                Port:    port.IntraClusterVxLAN,
    65	        }
    66	
    67	        logger.Infof("Deleting the %q interface", kp.vxlanIface)
    68	
    69	        err := netlinkAPI.New().LinkDel(iface)
    70	        if err != nil {
    71	                logger.Errorf(err, "Failed to delete the vxlan interface %q", kp.vxlanIface)
    72	        }
    73	}
    74	
    75	func (kp *SyncHandler) deleteIPTableChains() {
    76	        pFilter, err := packetfilter.New(kp.ipFamily)
    77	        if err != nil {
    78	                logger.Errorf(err, "Failed to initialize packetfilter interface")
    79	                return
    80	        }
    81	
    82	        logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmPostRouting, constants.NATTable)
    83	
    84	        if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeNAT, chains.SmPostRouting); err != nil {
    85	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing packetfilter chain %q of %q table", chains.SmPostRouting,
    86	                        constants.NATTable)
    87	        }
    88	
    89	        logger.Infof("Deleting IPv%v packetfilter entry in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmPostRouting, constants.NATTable)
    90	
    91	        chain := packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
    92	                Name:     chains.SmPostRouting,
    93	                Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeNAT,
    94	                Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookPostrouting,
    95	                Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityFirst,
    96	        }
    97	        if err := pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&chain); err != nil {
    98	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmPostRouting,
    99	                        constants.NATTable)
   100	        }
   101	
   102	        logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput, constants.FilterTable)
   103	
   104	        if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeFilter, chains.SmInput); err != nil {
   105	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing IPv%v packetfilter chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput,
   106	                        constants.FilterTable)
   107	        }
   108	
   109	        logger.Infof("Deleting IPv%v packetfilter entry in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput, constants.FilterTable)
   110	
   111	        chain = packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
   112	                Name:     chains.SmInput,
   113	                Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeFilter,
   114	                Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookInput,
   115	                Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityLast,
   116	                JumpRule: &packetfilter.Rule{
   117	                        Proto:       packetfilter.RuleProtoUDP,
   118	                        Action:      packetfilter.RuleActionJump,
   119	                        TargetChain: chains.SmInput,
   120	                },
   121	        }
   122	        if err := pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&chain); err != nil {
   123	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmInput,
   124	                        constants.FilterTable)
   125	        }
   126	
   127	        logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmSelfSnat, constants.NATTable)
   128	
   129	        if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeNAT, chains.SmSelfSnat); err != nil {
   130	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing IPv%v packetfilter chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmSelfSnat,
   131	                        constants.NATTable)
   132	        }
   133	
   134	        chain = packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
   135	                Name:     chains.SmSelfSnat,
   136	                Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeNAT,
   137	                Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookPostrouting,
   138	                Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityMiddle,
   139	        }
   140	
   141	        if err := pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&chain); err != nil {
   142	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmSelfSnat,
   143	                        constants.NATTable)
   144	        }
   145	
   146	        logger.Infof("Flushing IPv%v packetfilter entries in %q chain of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmForward, constants.FilterTable)
   147	
   148	        if err := pFilter.ClearChain(packetfilter.TableTypeFilter, chains.SmForward); err != nil {
   149	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error flushing IPv%v packetfilter chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmForward,
   150	                        constants.FilterTable)
   151	        }
   152	
   153	        chain = packetfilter.ChainIPHook{
   154	                Name:     chains.SmForward,
   155	                Type:     packetfilter.ChainTypeFilter,
   156	                Hook:     packetfilter.ChainHookForward,
   157	                Priority: packetfilter.ChainPriorityFirst,
   158	        }
   159	
   160	        if err := pFilter.DeleteIPHookChain(&chain); err != nil {
   161	                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting IPv%v IPHook chain %q of %q table", kp.ipFamily, chains.SmForward,
   162	                        constants.FilterTable)
   163	        }
   164	}
   165

    34	}
    35	
    36	var logger = log.Logger{Logger: logf.Log.WithName("CableDriver")}
    37	
    38	func NewVXLANCleanup() event.Handler {
    39	        return &vxlanCleanup{}
    40	}
    41	
    42	func (h *vxlanCleanup) GetNetworkPlugins() []string {
    43	        return []string{event.AnyNetworkPlugin}
    44	}

    46	func (h *vxlanCleanup) GetName() string {
    47	        return "VXLAN cleanup handler"
    48	}
    49	
    50	func (h *vxlanCleanup) TransitionToNonGateway() error {
    51	        logger.Infof("Cleaning up the routes")
    52	
    53	        errv6 := netlink.DeleteIfaceAndAssociatedRoutes(vxlan.GetVxlanInterfaceName(k8snet.IPv6), vxlan.TableID, k8snet.IPv6)
    54	        errv4 := netlink.DeleteIfaceAndAssociatedRoutes(vxlan.GetVxlanInterfaceName(k8snet.IPv4), vxlan.TableID, k8snet.IPv4)
    55	
    56	        return errors.Join(errv6, errv4)
    57	}

2026-05-29 23:36:30,368 - INFO - swea-agent-submariner-io__submariner-3957 - 💭 THOUGHT
Wait, there's a variable shadowing issue: `ctx` is passed as parameter, but then `ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, ...)` shadows it. Let me check if this was the original pattern. The original code used `context.Background()` which was fine because there was no context parameter. Now with the context parameter, we should use the passed-in context. But the shadowing is fine since we want to use the new context. Let me actually keep the original intent - the original used `context.Background()` but with the PR we want to use the caller's context:

Actually looking at the original code:
```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
```

With the new signature, the caller can impose a deadline. So I think the change should be:
```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 10*time.Second)
```

But wait, if the caller already passes a context with a deadline, adding another timeout might be redundant but it's safe. Actually, the PR description says "so callers can impose a deadline and/or cancel internal operations." So passing the caller's context through is the right thing. Let me verify the variable shadowing is fine - it is, since we create a new context with timeout from the passed one.

But actually there's another issue: the `gatewayMonitorInterface.Stop()` method also calls `g.monitor.Stop()` without a context:

```go
func (g *gatewayMonitorInterface) Stop() {
    _ = g.monitor.Stop()
```

 // Handlers that match the given networkPlugin name. The given event Handlers whose associated network plugin matches the given
 // networkPlugin name are added. Non-matching Handlers are ignored. Handlers will be called in registration order.

+                name:          name,
+                networkPlugin: strings.ToLower(networkPlugin),
+                eventHandlers: []Handler{},
+        }

+                err := r.addHandler(ctx, eventHandler)
+                if err != nil {
+                        return nil, err
+                }

+                return nil
+        case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
+                return fmt.Errorf("timeout sending event %q to channel", eventName)
+        }
 }

+        // We'll panic if config is nil, this is intentional
+        gatewayMonitor := &gatewayMonitor{
+                baseController:       newBaseController(),
+                GatewayMonitorConfig: *config,
+                shuttingDown:         atomic.Bool{},
+                leaderElectionInfo:   atomic.Pointer[LeaderElectionInfo]{},
+        }
+
+        // When transitioning to a non-gateway or shutting down, the GatewayMonitor cancels leader election which causes it to release the lock,
+        // which clears the HolderIdentity field and sets LeaseDuration to one second. This enables the next instance to quickly acquire the
+        // lock. However, if the controller instance crashes and doesn't properly release the lock, the next instance will have to await the
+        // LeaseDuration period before acquiring the lock. So we don't want LeaseDuration set too high, and we don't want it too low either to
+        // give the current instance enough time to complete stopping its controllers.
+        //
+        // The K8s leader election functionality periodically renews the lease. If it can't be renewed prior to the RenewDeadline, it stops.
+        // For our usage, we don't have instances concurrently vying for leadership, so we really don't need to keep renewing the lease. Ideally
+        // we would set the RenewDeadline very high to essentially disable it, but it needs to be less than the LeaseDuration setting which we
+        // don't want too high.
+
+        gatewayMonitor.LocalCIDRs = set.New(config.LocalCIDRs...).UnsortedList()
+
+        if gatewayMonitor.LeaseDuration == 0 {
+                gatewayMonitor.LeaseDuration = 20 * time.Second
+        }
+
+        if gatewayMonitor.RenewDeadline == 0 {
+                gatewayMonitor.RenewDeadline = 15 * time.Second
+        }
+
+        if gatewayMonitor.RetryPeriod == 0 {
+                gatewayMonitor.RetryPeriod = 2 * time.Second
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.leaderElectionInfo.Store(&LeaderElectionInfo{})
+
+        var err error
+
+        gatewayMonitor.pFilter, err = packetfilter.New(k8snet.IPv4)
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating packetfilter")
+        }
+
+        nodeName, ok := os.LookupEnv("NODE_NAME")
+        if !ok {
+                return nil, errors.New("error reading the NODE_NAME from the environment")
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.nodeName = nodeName
+
+        cniIface, err := cni.Discover(gatewayMonitor.LocalClusterCIDRs, k8snet.IPv4)
+        if err == nil {
+                gatewayMonitor.cniIP = cniIface.IPAddress
+
+                logger.Infof("Discovered CNI interface IP %q", gatewayMonitor.cniIP)
+        } else {
+                logger.Errorf(err, "Error obtaining CNI IP address - health check functionality will not work")
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.syncerConfig = &syncer.ResourceSyncerConfig{
+                SourceClient:    config.Client,
+                SourceNamespace: corev1.NamespaceAll,
+                Direction:       syncer.RemoteToLocal,
+                RestMapper:      config.RestMapper,
+                Federator: federate.NewCreateOrUpdateFederator(federate.CreateOrUpdateOptions{
+                        Client:          config.Client,
+                        RestMapper:      config.RestMapper,
+                        TargetNamespace: corev1.NamespaceAll,
+                }),
+                Scheme: config.Scheme,
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.gatewaySharedInformer, err = syncer.NewSharedInformer(
+                NewGatewayResourceSyncerConfig(gatewayMonitor.syncerConfig, config.Spec.Namespace))
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating shared informer")
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.gatewaySharedInformerStopCh = make(chan struct{})
+
+        registry, err := event.NewRegistry(ctx, "globalnet-registry", event.AnyNetworkPlugin, gatewayMonitor)
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating event registry")
+        }
+
+        return &gatewayMonitorInterface{monitor: gatewayMonitor, registry: registry}, nil
 }

-				close(leaderElectionInfo.stopped)
-
-				// We may have lost leadership due to failed renewal and not gateway node transition or shutdown, in which case we want to
-				// try to regain leadership. We also stop the controllers in case there's a gateway transition during the time that
-				// leadership is lost and a new instance is able to contact the API server to acquire the lock. This avoids a potential
-				// window where both instances are running should this instance finally observe the gateway transition. Note that we don't
-				// clear the globalnet chains to avoid datapath disruption should leadership be regained.
-				if !g.shuttingDown.Load() && g.State().IsOnGateway() {
-					g.stopControllers(context.Background(), false)
-					g.startLeaderElection()
-				}
-			},
-		},
-	})
+        if g.shuttingDown.Load() {
+                return
+        }
+
+        g.controllersMutex.Lock()
+        defer g.controllersMutex.Unlock()
+
+        // Usually when leadership is lost it's due to transition to a non-gateway however if it's due to renewal failure then we try to regain
+        // leadership in which case we'll still be on the gateway. Hence the isGatewayNode check here.
+        if !g.State().IsOnGateway() {
+                return
+        }
+
+        ctx, stop := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
+
+        leaderElectionInfo := &LeaderElectionInfo{
+                stopFunc: stop,
+                stopped:  make(chan struct{}),
+        }
+
+        g.leaderElectionInfo.Store(leaderElectionInfo)
+
+        logger.Info("On Gateway node - starting leader election")
+
+        lock, err := resourcelock.New(resourcelock.LeasesResourceLock, g.GatewayMonitorConfig.Spec.Namespace, LeaderElectionLockName,
+                g.KubeClient.CoreV1(), g.KubeClient.CoordinationV1(), resourcelock.ResourceLockConfig{
+                        Identity: g.nodeName + "-submariner-gateway",
+                })
+        utilruntime.Must(err)
+
+        go leaderelection.RunOrDie(ctx, leaderelection.LeaderElectionConfig{
+                Lock:            lock,
+                LeaseDuration:   g.LeaseDuration,
+                RenewDeadline:   g.RenewDeadline,
+                RetryPeriod:     g.RetryPeriod,
+                ReleaseOnCancel: true,
+                Callbacks: leaderelection.LeaderCallbacks{
+                        OnStartedLeading: func(_ context.Context) {
+                                err := g.startControllers() //nolint:contextcheck // Intentional to not pass context
+                                if err != nil {
+                                        logger.Error(err, "Error starting the controllers - stopping leader election")
+                                        leaderElectionInfo.stop()
+                                }
+                        },
+                        OnStoppedLeading: func() {
+                                logger.Info("Leader election stopped")
+
+                                close(leaderElectionInfo.stopped)
+
+                                // We may have lost leadership due to failed renewal and not gateway node transition or shutdown, in which case we want to
+                                // try to regain leadership. We also stop the controllers in case there's a gateway transition during the time that
+                                // leadership is lost and a new instance is able to contact the API server to acquire the lock. This avoids a potential
+                                // window where both instances are running should this instance finally observe the gateway transition. Note that we don't
+                                // clear the globalnet chains to avoid datapath disruption should leadership be regained.
+                                if !g.shuttingDown.Load() && g.State().IsOnGateway() {
+                                        g.stopControllers(context.Background(), false)
+                                        g.startLeaderElection()
+                                }
+                        },
+                },
+        })
 }

+        g.controllersMutex.Lock()
+        defer g.controllersMutex.Unlock()
+
+        // Since this is called asynchronously when leadership is gained, check that we're still on the gateway node and that we're
+        // not shutting down. Also, we may have regained leadership so ensure the controllers weren't already started.
+        if g.shuttingDown.Load() || !g.State().IsOnGateway() || len(g.controllers) > 0 {
+                return nil
+        }
+
+        logger.Info("Starting controllers")
+
+        err := g.createGlobalnetChains()
+        if err != nil {
+                return err
+        }
+
+        pool, err := ipam.NewIPPool(g.GatewayMonitorConfig.Spec.GlobalCIDR[0], metrics.GlobalnetMetricsReporter)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the IP pool")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = nil
+
+        c, err := NewClusterGlobalEgressIPController(g.syncerConfig, g.LocalCIDRs, pool)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the ClusterGlobalEgressIP controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+
+        c, err = NewGlobalEgressIPController(g.syncerConfig, pool)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the GlobalEgressIP controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+
+        // A user is not normally expected to delete the internal service created by the Globalnet controller.
+        // However, when it's accidentally done while the globalnet controller is down, the internal service
+        // remains until the finalizer is removed. We have seen that this intermediate state of
+        // the service can potentially create issues in some deployments. Hence, we identify such internal
+        // services and delete them when the Globalnet controller pod comes up.
+        err = RemoveStaleInternalServices(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                // Just log an error message as it's non-fatal
+                logger.Errorf(err, "Error removing stale internal services created by Globalnet controller")
+        }
+
+        // The GlobalIngressIP controller needs to be started before the ServiceExport and Service controllers to ensure
+        // reconciliation works properly.
+        gipController, err := NewGlobalIngressIPController(g.syncerConfig, pool)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the GlobalIngressIP controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, gipController)
+
+        podControllers, err := NewIngressPodControllers(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the IngressPodControllers")
+        }
+
+        endpointsControllers, err := NewServiceExportEndpointsControllers(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the Endpoints controller")
+        }
+
+        ingressEndpointsControllers, err := NewIngressEndpointsControllers(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.WithMessage(err, "error creating the IngressEndpointsControllers")
+        }
+
+        seController, err := NewServiceExportController(g.syncerConfig, podControllers, endpointsControllers,
+                ingressEndpointsControllers)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the ServiceExport controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, seController)
+
+        c, err = NewServiceController(g.syncerConfig, podControllers, seController.GetSyncer(), gipController.GetSyncer())
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the Service controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+
+        if g.cniIP != "" {
+                c, err := NewGatewayController(g.syncerConfig, g.gatewaySharedInformer, pool, g.Hostname, g.Spec.Namespace, g.cniIP)
+                if err != nil {
+                        return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the Gateway controller")
+                }
+
+                g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+        }
+
+        for _, c := range g.controllers {
+                err = c.Start()
+                if err != nil {
+                        return err //nolint:wrapcheck  // Let the caller wrap it
+                }
+        }
+
+        logger.Info("Successfully started the controllers")
+
+        return nil
 }

+                        }

+                        logger.V(log.DEBUG).Infof("Deleting rule that marks remote cluster traffic: %+v", &ruleSpec)
+
+                        if err := g.pFilter.Delete(packetfilter.TableTypeNAT, chains.SmGlobalnetMark, &ruleSpec); err != nil {
+                                logger.Errorf(err, "Error deleting iptables rule %+v", &ruleSpec)
+                        }
+                }
+        }
 }

-	if l.stopFunc != nil {
-		l.stopFunc()
-	}
+        if l.stopFunc != nil {
+                l.stopFunc()
+        }
 }

+        }

+        case <-ctx.Done():

+        }
 }

-

+        // We'll panic if config is nil, this is intentional
+        gatewayMonitor := &gatewayMonitor{
+                baseController:       newBaseController(),
+                GatewayMonitorConfig: *config,
+                shuttingDown:         atomic.Bool{},
+                leaderElectionInfo:   atomic.Pointer[LeaderElectionInfo]{},
+        }
+
+        // When transitioning to a non-gateway or shutting down, the GatewayMonitor cancels leader election which causes it to release the lock,
+        // which clears the HolderIdentity field and sets LeaseDuration to one second. This enables the next instance to quickly acquire the
+        // lock. However, if the controller instance crashes and doesn't properly release the lock, the next instance will have to await the
+        // LeaseDuration period before acquiring the lock. So we don't want LeaseDuration set too high, and we don't want it too low either to
+        // give the current instance enough time to complete stopping its controllers.
+        //
+        // The K8s leader election functionality periodically renews the lease. If it can't be renewed prior to the RenewDeadline, it stops.
+        // For our usage, we don't have instances concurrently vying for leadership, so we really don't need to keep renewing the lease. Ideally
+        // we would set the RenewDeadline very high to essentially disable it, but it needs to be less than the LeaseDuration setting which we
+        // don't want too high.
+
+        gatewayMonitor.LocalCIDRs = set.New(config.LocalCIDRs...).UnsortedList()
+
+        if gatewayMonitor.LeaseDuration == 0 {
+                gatewayMonitor.LeaseDuration = 20 * time.Second
+        }
+
+        if gatewayMonitor.RenewDeadline == 0 {
+                gatewayMonitor.RenewDeadline = 15 * time.Second
+        }
+
+        if gatewayMonitor.RetryPeriod == 0 {
+                gatewayMonitor.RetryPeriod = 2 * time.Second
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.leaderElectionInfo.Store(&LeaderElectionInfo{})
+
+        var err error
+
+        gatewayMonitor.pFilter, err = packetfilter.New(k8snet.IPv4)
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating packetfilter")
+        }
+
+        nodeName, ok := os.LookupEnv("NODE_NAME")
+        if !ok {
+                return nil, errors.New("error reading the NODE_NAME from the environment")
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.nodeName = nodeName
+
+        cniIface, err := cni.Discover(gatewayMonitor.LocalClusterCIDRs, k8snet.IPv4)
+        if err == nil {
+                gatewayMonitor.cniIP = cniIface.IPAddress
+
+                logger.Infof("Discovered CNI interface IP %q", gatewayMonitor.cniIP)
+        } else {
+                logger.Errorf(err, "Error obtaining CNI IP address - health check functionality will not work")
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.syncerConfig = &syncer.ResourceSyncerConfig{
+                SourceClient:    config.Client,
+                SourceNamespace: corev1.NamespaceAll,
+                Direction:       syncer.RemoteToLocal,
+                RestMapper:      config.RestMapper,
+                Federator: federate.NewCreateOrUpdateFederator(federate.CreateOrUpdateOptions{
+                        Client:          config.Client,
+                        RestMapper:      config.RestMapper,
+                        TargetNamespace: corev1.NamespaceAll,
+                }),
+                Scheme: config.Scheme,
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.gatewaySharedInformer, err = syncer.NewSharedInformer(
+                NewGatewayResourceSyncerConfig(gatewayMonitor.syncerConfig, config.Spec.Namespace))
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating shared informer")
+        }
+
+        gatewayMonitor.gatewaySharedInformerStopCh = make(chan struct{})
+
+        registry, err := event.NewRegistry(ctx, "globalnet-registry", event.AnyNetworkPlugin, gatewayMonitor)
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating event registry")
+        }
+
+        return &gatewayMonitorInterface{monitor: gatewayMonitor, registry: registry}, nil
 }

+        err := g.monitor.startLocalGatewayCleanupController()

-	// leadership in which case we'll still be on the gateway. Hence the isGatewayNode check here.

-			},
-			OnStoppedLeading: func() {
-				logger.Info("Leader election stopped")
-
-				close(leaderElectionInfo.stopped)
-
-				// We may have lost leadership due to failed renewal and not gateway node transition or shutdown, in which case we want to
-				// try to regain leadership. We also stop the controllers in case there's a gateway transition during the time that
-				// leadership is lost and a new instance is able to contact the API server to acquire the lock. This avoids a potential
-				// window where both instances are running should this instance finally observe the gateway transition. Note that we don't

-				if !g.shuttingDown.Load() && g.State().IsOnGateway() {

-	})
+        if g.shuttingDown.Load() {
+                return
+        }
+
+        g.controllersMutex.Lock()
+        defer g.controllersMutex.Unlock()
+
+        // Usually when leadership is lost it's due to transition to a non-gateway however if it's due to renewal failure then we try to regain
+        // leadership in which case we'll still be on the gateway. Hence the isGatewayNode check here.
+        if !g.State().IsOnGateway() {
+                return
+        }
+
+        ctx, stop := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
+
+        leaderElectionInfo := &LeaderElectionInfo{
+                stopFunc: stop,
+                stopped:  make(chan struct{}),
+        }
+
+        g.leaderElectionInfo.Store(leaderElectionInfo)
+
+        logger.Info("On Gateway node - starting leader election")
+
+        lock, err := resourcelock.New(resourcelock.LeasesResourceLock, g.GatewayMonitorConfig.Spec.Namespace, LeaderElectionLockName,
+                g.KubeClient.CoreV1(), g.KubeClient.CoordinationV1(), resourcelock.ResourceLockConfig{
+                        Identity: g.nodeName + "-submariner-gateway",
+                })
+        utilruntime.Must(err)
+
+        go leaderelection.RunOrDie(ctx, leaderelection.LeaderElectionConfig{
+                Lock:            lock,
+                LeaseDuration:   g.LeaseDuration,
+                RenewDeadline:   g.RenewDeadline,
+                RetryPeriod:     g.RetryPeriod,
+                ReleaseOnCancel: true,
+                Callbacks: leaderelection.LeaderCallbacks{
+                        OnStartedLeading: func(_ context.Context) {
+                                err := g.startControllers() //nolint:contextcheck // Intentional to not pass context
+                                if err != nil {
+                                        logger.Error(err, "Error starting the controllers - stopping leader election")
+                                        leaderElectionInfo.stop()
+                                }
+                        },
+                        OnStoppedLeading: func() {
+                                logger.Info("Leader election stopped")
+
+                                close(leaderElectionInfo.stopped)
+
+                                // We may have lost leadership due to failed renewal and not gateway node transition or shutdown, in which case we want to
+                                // try to regain leadership. We also stop the controllers in case there's a gateway transition during the time that
+                                // leadership is lost and a new instance is able to contact the API server to acquire the lock. This avoids a potential
+                                // window where both instances are running should this instance finally observe the gateway transition. Note that we don't
+                                // clear the globalnet chains to avoid datapath disruption should leadership be regained.
+                                if !g.shuttingDown.Load() && g.State().IsOnGateway() {
+                                        g.stopControllers(context.Background(), false)
+                                        g.startLeaderElection()
+                                }
+                        },
+                },
+        })
 }

+        err := g.createGlobalnetChains()
+        if err != nil {
+                return err
+        }
+
+        pool, err := ipam.NewIPPool(g.GatewayMonitorConfig.Spec.GlobalCIDR[0], metrics.GlobalnetMetricsReporter)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the IP pool")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = nil
+
+        c, err := NewClusterGlobalEgressIPController(g.syncerConfig, g.LocalCIDRs, pool)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the ClusterGlobalEgressIP controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+
+        c, err = NewGlobalEgressIPController(g.syncerConfig, pool)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the GlobalEgressIP controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+
+        // A user is not normally expected to delete the internal service created by the Globalnet controller.
+        // However, when it's accidentally done while the globalnet controller is down, the internal service
+        // remains until the finalizer is removed. We have seen that this intermediate state of
+        // the service can potentially create issues in some deployments. Hence, we identify such internal
+        // services and delete them when the Globalnet controller pod comes up.
+        err = RemoveStaleInternalServices(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                // Just log an error message as it's non-fatal
+                logger.Errorf(err, "Error removing stale internal services created by Globalnet controller")
+        }
+
+        // The GlobalIngressIP controller needs to be started before the ServiceExport and Service controllers to ensure
+        // reconciliation works properly.
+        gipController, err := NewGlobalIngressIPController(g.syncerConfig, pool)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the GlobalIngressIP controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, gipController)
+
+        podControllers, err := NewIngressPodControllers(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the IngressPodControllers")
+        }
+
+        endpointsControllers, err := NewServiceExportEndpointsControllers(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the Endpoints controller")
+        }
+
+        ingressEndpointsControllers, err := NewIngressEndpointsControllers(g.syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.WithMessage(err, "error creating the IngressEndpointsControllers")
+        }
+
+        seController, err := NewServiceExportController(g.syncerConfig, podControllers, endpointsControllers,
+                ingressEndpointsControllers)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the ServiceExport controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, seController)
+
+        c, err = NewServiceController(g.syncerConfig, podControllers, seController.GetSyncer(), gipController.GetSyncer())
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the Service controller")
+        }
+
+        g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+
+        if g.cniIP != "" {
+                c, err := NewGatewayController(g.syncerConfig, g.gatewaySharedInformer, pool, g.Hostname, g.Spec.Namespace, g.cniIP)
+                if err != nil {
+                        return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating the Gateway controller")
+                }
+
+                g.controllers = append(g.controllers, c)
+        }
+
+        for _, c := range g.controllers {
+                err = c.Start()
+                if err != nil {
+                        return err //nolint:wrapcheck  // Let the caller wrap it
+                }
+        }
+
+        logger.Info("Successfully started the controllers")
+
+        return nil
 }

+        g.controllersMutex.Lock()

-	// An mtuProbe value of 2 enables PLPMTUD. Along with this change, we also configure
-	// base mss to 1024 as per RFC4821 recommendation.

-	baseMss := "1024"

-	// on Gateway node has mtu issues connecting to remoteServices.

+        // base mss to 1024 as per RFC4821 recommendation.
+        mtuProbe := "2"
+        baseMss := "1024"
+
+        // If we are unable to update the values, just log a warning. Most of the Globalnet
+        // functionality works fine except for one use-case where Pod with HostNetworking
+        // on Gateway node has mtu issues connecting to remoteServices.
+        err := netlink.New().ConfigureTCPMTUProbe(mtuProbe, baseMss)
+        if err != nil {
+                logger.Warning(err.Error())
+        }
 }

+        }
+
+        ctl.endpointInformer, err = syncer.NewSharedInformer(syncerConfig)
+        if err != nil {
+                return nil, errors.Wrap(err, "error creating endpoint informer")
+        }
+
+        for _, handler := range config.Registry.GetHandlers() {
+                err = ctl.setupHandlerController(handler, env.ClusterID, hostname, *syncerConfig)
+                if err != nil {
+                        return nil, err
+                }
+        }
+
+        return &ctl, nil
 }

+        hCtrl := &handlerController{
+                handler:            handler,
+                hostname:           hostname,
+                clusterID:          clusterID,
+                lastRemoteEndpoint: map[string]*subv1.Endpoint{},
+        }

+
+        syncerConfig.Transform = func(obj runtime.Object, numRequeues int, op syncer.Operation) (runtime.Object, bool) {
+                switch op {
+                case syncer.Create:
+                        return nil, hCtrl.handleCreatedEndpoint(obj.(*subv1.Endpoint), numRequeues)
+                case syncer.Update:
+                        return nil, hCtrl.handleUpdatedEndpoint(obj.(*subv1.Endpoint), numRequeues)
+                case syncer.Delete:
+                        return nil, hCtrl.handleRemovedEndpoint(obj.(*subv1.Endpoint), numRequeues)
+                }
+
+                return nil, false
+        }
+
+        endpointSyncer, err := syncer.NewResourceSyncerWithSharedInformer(&syncerConfig, c.endpointInformer)
+        if err != nil {
+                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating resource syncer")
+        }
+
+        c.syncers = append(c.syncers, endpointSyncer)
+
+        var ok bool
+
+        hCtrl.nodeHandler, ok = hCtrl.handler.(event.NodeHandler)
+        if ok {
+                syncerConfig.Name = fmt.Sprintf("Node watcher for handler %q", handler.GetName())
+                syncerConfig.ResourceType = &k8sv1.Node{}
+                syncerConfig.SourceNamespace = k8sv1.NamespaceAll
+
+                if c.nodeInformer == nil {
+                        c.nodeInformer, err = syncer.NewSharedInformer(&syncerConfig)
+                        if err != nil {
+                                return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating node informer")
+                        }
+                }
+
+                syncerConfig.Transform = func(obj runtime.Object, numRequeues int, op syncer.Operation) (runtime.Object, bool) {
+                        switch op {
+                        case syncer.Create:
+                                return nil, hCtrl.handleCreatedNode(obj.(*k8sv1.Node), numRequeues)
+                        case syncer.Update:
+                                return nil, hCtrl.handleUpdatedNode(obj.(*k8sv1.Node), numRequeues)
+                        case syncer.Delete:
+                                return nil, hCtrl.handleRemovedNode(obj.(*k8sv1.Node), numRequeues)
+                        }
+
+                        return nil, false
+                }
+
+                nodeSyncer, err := syncer.NewResourceSyncerWithSharedInformer(&syncerConfig, c.nodeInformer)
+                if err != nil {
+                        return errors.Wrap(err, "error creating resource syncer")
+                }
+
+                c.syncers = append(c.syncers, nodeSyncer)
+        }
+
+        return nil
 }

+                go func() {
+                        informer.Run(stopCh)
+                }()
+        }
 }
